description: Detects basic accessibility violations in HTML/JSX — WCAG 2.1 Level AA, IS 17802, RPwD Act §40

patterns.name: Image missing alt attribute
patterns.description: img element with no alt attribute. Screen readers cannot describe the image to visually impaired users.
patterns.note: Matches img tags that do not have alt= anywhere in their attributes.

patterns.name: HTML document missing lang attribute
patterns.description: The root html element must declare the document language for screen readers.
patterns.note: Check the main HTML template or document root. In React/Next.js check _document.js or the html element in the root layout.

patterns.name: Input with placeholder but no label
patterns.description: Form input using only placeholder for labeling — placeholder disappears when typing and is not accessible to all assistive technologies.
patterns.note: A separate check for associated label tags is done in the file-level analysis. This catches inputs with only placeholder and no id (so no label can be associated).

patterns.name: Keyboard focus outline suppressed
patterns.description: CSS removing the focus outline without replacement is a keyboard accessibility barrier. Visible focus is required by WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7.
patterns.note: Flag these locations for manual verification — outline:none is acceptable only if a custom focus indicator is provided (e.g., custom outline or box-shadow on :focus-visible).

patterns.name: Interactive div/span without role or tabindex
patterns.description: Div or span used as a button/link (with onClick) but without role='button' and keyboard event handling — not accessible by keyboard.
patterns.note: Replace with semantic <button> or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler. Low severity as it may have keyboard handlers elsewhere.
